close_reason: skip_string_b is a byte-level skip function that mirrors the char-level json_skip_string. Both handle \uXXXX escapes correctly for their purpose (skipping past the escape sequence). Surrogate pair validation is a JSON spec conformance issue, not a correctness bug — the function correctly advances past the 6-byte \uXXXX sequence regardless of the code point value.
description: In secretx-core/src/lib.rs, the byte-level JSON scanner skip_string_b (used by extract_path / extract_path_field for navigation) handles \uXXXX escape sequences (lines 846-860) by consuming exactly 4 hex bytes without checking for surrogate pairs (0xD800-0xDFFF). An isolated high surrogate (e.g. \uD800 without a following \uDC00) is consumed silently as if valid, which means the scanner can desync from the true JSON structure if the string contains malformed surrogate escapes. The char-level scanner json_skip_string (lines 663-720) validates surrogates correctly and returns Err on isolated surrogates. The asymmetry means extract_path on a JSON value containing a lone surrogate in a key name could navigate incorrectly. This is a niche case (RFC 8259 forbids lone surrogates in conforming JSON) but documented as a known gap. Consider aligning skip_string_b with json_skip_string's validation, or adding a comment cross-referencing the asymmetry to reduce future maintenance risk.
title: core: skip_string_b skips \uXXXX without surrogate pair validation
